raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing an XXXX charge for a replacement scanner that I never received and did not authorize as a purchase. XXXX took my card only as a hold, not as consent to bill me. Please remove this charge immediately and issue provisional credit while you investigate pursuant to the Fair Credit Billing Act.

What happened I originally purchased my scanner for about {$400.00} ( receipt attached ).

After a 3-hour support call, XXXX failed to diagnose a simple feed issue I later resolved myself. 
XXXX captured my American Express card strictly as a hold to send a replacement. I cancelled once the issue was resolved. 
Despite my cancellation and XXXX confirmation that the charge would be refunded, my card was still billed ( charge ~ {$250.00} on or about XX/XX/XXXX ; statement page attached ). 
XXXX now claims the unit was delivered, but there is no delivery photo, no signature, and I did not receive any package. 
As a result, I have now paid roughly {$700.00} in connection with a single scanner, which is unreasonable and contrary to XXXX own representations. 

Why this qualifies for reversal Merchandise Not Received : No signed proof of delivery or photo ; I never took possession.

Misrepresentation/Unauthorized Billing : The card was supplied as a security hold, not permission to convert to a sale. 
Vendor Promised Refund : XXXX confirmed a refund would be issued ; it was not.

Consumer Burden : If XXXX believes it shipped the item, the correct path is for XXXX to file a lost-package claim with the carriernot to shift the loss onto me.
